source_pr.title: ddl: support modify column on partitioned table
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #38297

Problem Summary:

Support for partitioned tables was never added for MODIFY COLUMN.

### What is changed and how it works?

Removed the limitation and added handling of partitions for the column change DDL operation. Updating indexes was already supported for partitioned tables, so only the table data / rows handling needed to be updated.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [X] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [X] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
Support `ALTER TABLE t MODIFY column...` on partitioned table
```

symptoms: Feature change (support ALTER TABLE ... MODIFY COLUMN on partitioned tables) plus new/updated tests; no concrete flaky failure signature is captured in this case.
failure_signature: No explicit flaky failure signature in patch (feature + tests).
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: The patch removes the limitation for MODIFY COLUMN on partitioned tables and adds DDL backfill handling plus new tests. It does not provide a concrete intermittent flaky failure signature or a targeted stabilization fix, so keep this case as needs-more-evidence.
fix_pattern: Support MODIFY COLUMN on partitioned tables (update backfill/row handling) and add tests.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery(`select * from t order by b`).Check(testkit.Rows(""+ (ddl/db_partition_test.go)
files: ddl/backfilling.go, ddl/column.go, ddl/db_partition_test.go, ddl/ddl_api.go, ddl/failtest/fail_db_test.go
